overview.description: A tooltip component for displaying informative text when users hover over, focus on, or tap an element.
overview.purpose: A tooltip component for displaying informative text when users hover over, focus on, or tap an element.
whenToUse: When you need to provide additional information about a UI element on hover
whenNotToUse: When the information is critical and must be always visible
consider using a different UI pattern.

examples.basic: <mc-tooltip opendelay="50" position="top-left" width="auto" appearance="neutral-default" fit="medium" trigger="hover focus click" zindex="99999"></mc-tooltip>

advanced.InteractiveContentWithButton:
// JavaScript import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tooltip'; // HTML
Interactive content

props.name: opendelay
props.description: Delay(in ms) after which the tooltip will open. Only supported when the trigger is set to `hover`. Defaults to 50

props.name: position
props.description: Specifies the position of the tooltip in regards to the target element

props.name: width
props.description: The max width of the tooltip in pixels. If not provided the width will be `auto`

props.name: appearance
props.description: If not specified, the default value is: neutral-default

props.name: trigger
props.description: Determines the events that cause the mc-tooltip to show. Multiple event names are separated by spaces.

props.name: customtriggerelement
props.description: HTML element or element ID (string) to attach the popover to. When a string is provided, the element will be found using document.getElementById(). Can be used instead of `trigger` named slot for performance optimization (e.g., large tables).

props.name: open
props.description: When set to true, tooltip will be visible

props.name: zindex
props.description: Set only set if two tooltips or a tooltip and another z-indexed panel need to overlap.

slots.description: Use the default slot, to supply the HTML content. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
Use the trigger named slot, to specify the element which toggles the popover. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: trigger

members.name: position
members.description: Specifies the position of the tooltip in regards to the target element.

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the popover container. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: popover-container

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the popover arrow. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: popover-arrow

guidelines.title: Tooltip
guidelines.description: A tooltip is a descriptive text box that is used to provide contextual information about a UI element.

best-practice: The tooltip should always be placed in close proximity to the element it describes.
Only use a tooltip when additional information is needed to understand the UI.
Use tooltips to describe the difference between very similar elements.
usage: A tooltip component is a user interface element that displays a short message when the user hovers over, focuses on, or taps an element (e.g. icon or text). The message usually provides additional information or guidance about the element, such as its function or purpose. A tooltip component can be used to enhance the usability and accessibility of a web page or application.
use-within-text: Use tooltip when the element has a label that is not clear or descriptive enough, and you want to explain what it does or what it means. For example, use a tooltip component to show the full name of an acronym, the definition of a term, or the description of a feature.

relatedGuidelines.title: Accessibility
relatedGuidelines.description: Improving the user experience for everyone.

what-is-accessibility?: Accessibility means that websites, tools and technologies are designed and developed in a way that makes it possible for people with disabilities to use them. Accessibility also benefits people without disabilities for example people with temporary disabilities or situational disabilities.
Accessibility helps people perceive, understand and interact with our digital products and it takes auditory, cognitive, neurological, physical, speech and visual limitations into consideration.

But accessibility is also a mindset and a change in the way we think and talk about digital products. It is a cultural change that cannot be made by one team only. We, therefore, urge all our colleagues as well as top management to get involved and share awareness about this very important topic. We need your support!
why-is-accessibility-important?: One of the main myths about accessibility is that it only concerns a small group of people with severe disabilities. This assumption is far from the reality and according to the World Health Organisation (WHO), more than 1 billion people are living with some level of impairment that affects the way they interact with digital products and access information online.
When our websites, apps and other digital products are designed badly, they create barriers that exclude people and make access to our offerings cumbersome and in some cases not possible at all.
being-inaccessible-is-a-problem: It is an ethical problem when we exclude certain groups of people from our products and services.

It is a legal problem, especially in the US where companies are being sued if they are not accessible, which could have financial consequences in terms of fines as well as affecting the brand negatively.

It is a big business problem - if we choose to exclude a very big group of potential customers because they have a disability.
When we design and build products with accessibility in mind, we not only open up our products and experiences for more people, but our products start to become more human - in the sense that they reflect the diversity of society and become much more flexible and robust to accommodate many different needs and use cases.
